description: Third-party plugins to install via each platform's native plugin system

plugins.name: earthchen-ai-assets
plugins.description: This repo's own plugin — deployed to Claude Code via marketplace clone.

claude.note: Self-plugin; provides skills, agents, MCP on Claude.

plugins.name: mattpocock-skills
plugins.description: Engineering skills by Matt Pocock (mattpocock/skills). Hybrid management: Claude uses the native plugin; Codex/Cursor use manual symlinks into ~/.agents/skills/.

claude.note: Native plugin is the sole source of mattpocock skills on Claude; this repo's build excludes them from its Claude distribution. NOT installed by `install.py manual` on Claude.

codex.note: Installed by `install.py manual mattpocock-skills` — generates 22 symlinks into ~/.agents/skills/ (Codex standard user path) from the upstream plugin.json skill list. Codex follows symlinks per loader.rs (User scope = Follow).

cursor.note: Same as codex: `install.py manual mattpocock-skills` → ~/.agents/skills/. Cursor scans ~/.agents/skills/ by default. Known bug: home-dir skill symlinks may vanish after restart (v2.5.x); if hit, copy the folders instead of symlinking.

plugins.name: anysearch
plugins.description: Unified real-time search engine skill (web/vertical/batch/extract). Replaces exa MCP — anysearch is a CLI skill calling api.anysearch.com, NOT an MCP server. NOT plugin-distributed: needs persistent runtime.conf/.env that plugin cache (a read-only snapshot overwritten on every ref:main pull) cannot hold.

claude.note: ONLY user-level path Claude scans (Claude does NOT read ~/.agents/skills/). Submodule at vendor/anysearch-skill (pinned v2.1.0) stays out of build/_dist — no skills/ symlink, no _deep_copy_skills entry.

codex.note: Codex follows symlinks per loader.rs (User scope = Follow). ~/.codex/skills is deprecated legacy; ~/.agents/skills is the standard.

cursor.note: Cursor scans ~/.agents/skills/ by default. Known bug: home-dir skill symlinks may vanish after restart (v2.5.x); if hit, copy the folder instead of symlinking.
